title: I Love Lisa

jokes.text: Radio DJ plays monster movie theme as Valentine's Day love song, then gets called out by co-host
jokes.setup: DJ confidently presents a monster movie song as romantic Valentine's music
jokes.punchline: Co-host reveals he just played the wrong record entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with good setup/payoff structure. The rationalization about monsters 'holding their evil in check' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Grampa: 'Bah! This is just another Hallmark holiday cooked up to sell cards.' [beat] 'Ohh. A valentine from my granddaughter.'
jokes.setup: Grampa cynically dismisses Valentine's Day as commercial manipulation
jokes.punchline: Immediately melts when he receives a valentine himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Grampa's hypocrisy. Excellent economy and structure with the immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: Moe receiving valentine 'from your secret admirer' in meek voice, followed by Barney's romantic advances
jokes.setup: Moe gets excited about a secret admirer
jokes.punchline: It's from pathetic Barney who immediately appears with romantic intentions
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor with Barney's pathetic romanticism and Moe's brief hope being crushed.

jokes.text: Homer panicking about forgetting Valentine's Day: 'Is it bacon day? No. That's crazy talk.'
jokes.setup: Homer frantically trying to remember what special day he forgot
jokes.punchline: His first guess is the nonsensical 'bacon day' which he then dismisses as crazy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his food obsession leads to absurd 'bacon day' guess, then he has just enough self-awareness to call it crazy talk.

jokes.text: Nobody makes a big deal out of Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Homer claims Valentine's Day isn't a big deal
jokes.punchline: Cut to elaborate romantic displays everywhere proving him wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic visual contradiction - reality immediately contradicts character's claim

jokes.text: Homer claims gift is 'upstairs, on the second floor of our house' then runs away
jokes.setup: Homer stalls for time by being overly specific about gift location
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary detail reveals he's lying and has to run away to get something
jokes.explanation: Homer's nervous over-specification combined with physical escape comedy. The redundant detail signals he's making it up.

jokes.text: Apu selling Valentine's gift for $100: 'Oh, I think you will' when Homer refuses to pay
jokes.setup: Homer refuses to pay outrageous price for last-minute gift
jokes.punchline: Apu confidently predicts Homer's desperation will win out
jokes.explanation: Apu's calm confidence about exploiting Homer's desperation. Good character moment showing Apu's shrewd business sense.

jokes.text: You saved my life. How much? One hundred dollars. What? That's highway robbery. I won't pay it.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately grateful to Apu for valentine solution
jokes.punchline: Immediately outraged at price despite life-saving claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - extreme gratitude instantly replaced by cheapness

jokes.text: Apu's internal monologue: 'If he discovers the discount supermarket next door, all is lost'
jokes.setup: Homer storms off saying he'll never shop there again
jokes.punchline: Apu's thoughts reveal his real fear is losing customers to cheaper competition
jokes.explanation: Shows Apu's business anxiety and the reality of small retail competition. The internal monologue format adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Miss Hoover responds to student questioning busywork: 'Bull's-eye. Get cracking.'
jokes.setup: Student astutely identifies Valentine's craft as pointless busywork
jokes.punchline: Teacher bluntly confirms it's meaningless but orders them to do it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Miss Hoover moment - honest about the meaninglessness of school activities but still enforcing them. Great deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: 'The children are right to laugh at you, Ralph' - Miss Hoover's brutal honesty
jokes.setup: Kids laugh at Ralph for not being allowed to use scissors
jokes.punchline: Teacher validates the mockery instead of protecting the student
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of teacher protection instincts. Miss Hoover's brutal honesty makes it shocking and memorable.

jokes.text: Ralph ate his red crayon, explaining why he doesn't have one
jokes.setup: Teacher asks why Ralph doesn't have a red crayon
jokes.punchline: Ralph matter-of-factly explains he ate it
jokes.explanation: Peak Ralph Wiggum absurdity delivered with his characteristic innocent matter-of-factness. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Skinner's announcement about 'crude, off-color sentiments' on candy hearts, 'possibly Bart Simpson'
jokes.setup: Formal announcement about inappropriate Valentine's candy
jokes.punchline: Skinner's barely-disguised accusation of Bart specifically
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment with his passive-aggressive targeting of Bart while maintaining official neutrality.

jokes.text: Bart watching violent TV: 'Cool! I broke his brain.'
jokes.setup: Character on TV appears traumatized by violence
jokes.punchline: Bart takes proud credit for the psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment showing his casual attitude toward psychological violence. The phrase 'broke his brain' is memorable.

jokes.text: Ralph: 'I glued my head to my shoulder' delivered weakly
jokes.setup: Class craft time with potential for mishaps
jokes.punchline: Ralph manages to glue his head to his shoulder in impossible accident
jokes.explanation: Absurd physical comedy that only Ralph could accomplish. The weak delivery makes it funnier as he seems resigned to his fate.

jokes.text: Lisa takes pity on Ralph who received no valentines
jokes.setup: Ralph sits alone with no valentines while others exchange happily
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pity valentine sets up the entire episode's central conflict
jokes.explanation: Critical plot moment that generates sympathy and sets up the episode's emotional stakes. Strong character work from Lisa.

jokes.text: Ralph: 'You choo-choo-choose me?' - reading Lisa's train valentine literally
jokes.setup: Lisa gives Ralph a valentine with a train and 'choo-choo-choose' pun
jokes.punchline: Ralph reads the pun literally with innocent wonder
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons moment. The pun works on multiple levels and Ralph's innocent delivery makes it both sweet and funny. Became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: It doesn't look very clean. Just do your job, heart boy.
jokes.setup: Valentine's Day lunch preparation with beef hearts
jokes.punchline: Dismissive nickname 'heart boy' for legitimate hygiene concern
jokes.explanation: School lunch quality joke - authority figure dismisses health concerns

jokes.text: Bart's fake medical emergency: 'My baboon heart. Body... rejecting it.'
jokes.setup: Bart appears to be having a medical crisis during lunch
jokes.punchline: Claims he has a baboon heart that's being rejected
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical humor with Bart's creative excuse-making. The baboon heart is specifically absurd and delivered with medical drama parody.

jokes.text: It says, 'Choo-choo-choose me...' and there's a picture of a train. Yeah. Nice gag.
jokes.setup: Ralph excitedly explains the valentine pun to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's uncomfortable 'nice gag' response to her pity gift
jokes.explanation: Awkward callback - Ralph's joy contrasts with Lisa's growing discomfort

jokes.text: Ralph's awkward conversation: 'do you like... stuff?'
jokes.setup: Ralph trying to make conversation while walking Lisa home
jokes.punchline: His attempt at small talk is painfully vague and awkward
jokes.explanation: Perfect capture of childhood romantic awkwardness. Ralph's vague question shows his social ineptitude in an endearing way.

jokes.text: Ralph oversharing about nosebleeds and finger-picking
jokes.setup: Continued awkward conversation on walk home
jokes.punchline: Ralph shares grossly inappropriate medical details as small talk
jokes.explanation: Ralph's characteristic oversharing of inappropriate details. Good cringe comedy showing his complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: The doctor said I wouldn't have so many nosebleeds if I kept my finger out of there.
jokes.setup: Ralph continues awkward conversation attempts
jokes.punchline: Overshares gross medical advice
jokes.explanation: Ralph's inappropriate sharing - combines gross-out humor with social ineptitude

jokes.text: Everyone's always kissing your ass. Well, I'm not afraid to tell you you're a-- [Bleep]
jokes.setup: Krusty shows clip of Sideshow Mel 'whacked out on wowee sauce'
jokes.punchline: Drunk sidekick finally speaks truth to clown power
jokes.explanation: Behind-scenes rebellion - sidekick's substance-fueled honesty about show dynamics

jokes.text: Sideshow Mel censored on Krusty show: 'You're a--[bleep]'
jokes.setup: Anniversary show clip of Sideshow Mel 'whacked out on wowee sauce'
jokes.punchline: His criticism of Krusty gets bleeped at the crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Standard TV censorship comedy. The buildup suggests something shocking but the bleep prevents the payoff.

jokes.text: Homer threatening to sell Bart: 'You'll do as you're told'
jokes.setup: Bart saying he'd sell his firstborn son for Krusty tickets
jokes.punchline: Homer takes the offer seriously and claims authority over Bart
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual consideration of child trafficking as viable option. Dark humor that plays on his paternal authority.

jokes.text: Grampa's wisdom about pity in romance: 'Ah, sweet pity. Where would my love life have been without it?'
jokes.setup: Lisa confessing she only gave Ralph valentine out of pity
jokes.punchline: Grampa fondly reminisces about pity as romantic foundation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Grampa moment combining self-deprecation with romantic wisdom. The wistful delivery makes it both funny and touching.

jokes.text: I like you as a friend. I think we should see other people. I no speak English.
jokes.setup: Homer lists rejection lines he's heard
jokes.punchline: Escalates from polite to desperate fake language barrier
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of rejection excuses - builds from standard to absurd

jokes.text: Homer's escalating rejection phrases ending with 'I'm not gay, but I'll learn'
jokes.setup: Homer listing ways to reject romantic advances
jokes.punchline: Final absurd rejection that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation comedy with increasingly absurd rejections. The final line is memorably nonsensical and became quotable.

jokes.text: Homer's advice about persistence while violently attacking a jar, then shooting it
jokes.setup: Homer giving calm advice about persistence and keeping your cool
jokes.punchline: Immediately loses his temper and shoots the jar he's trying to open
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer hypocrisy with perfect timing. The contrast between his advice and actions creates excellent ironic comedy.

jokes.text: Krusty's dark anniversary humor: 'And you know where those reviewers are? All dead.'
jokes.setup: Krusty celebrating 29 years despite critics predicting failure
jokes.punchline: Morbidly noting all his critics are now dead
jokes.explanation: Krusty's characteristic dark humor mixed with showbiz narcissism. Good character moment showing his morbid perspective on longevity.

jokes.text: Krusty's 'urine monkey' bit with dawning realization
jokes.setup: Krusty asks why they call something a 'urine monkey'
jokes.punchline: His disgusted reaction suggests he just found out firsthand
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with delayed realization structure. Works on the visual comedy of Krusty's horrified discovery.

jokes.text: Homer finding perfectly good toothbrush in Flanders' trash: 'Eww.'
jokes.setup: Homer scavenging through neighbor's trash for useful items
jokes.punchline: Despite being willing to take discarded items, he draws line at used toothbrush
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selective standards about hygiene even while dumpster diving.

jokes.text: Bart's crude response to Ralph: 'She's in the can. Go away.'
jokes.setup: Ralph comes to visit Lisa at the door
jokes.punchline: Bart's deliberately crude and dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Bart being characteristically rude and protective of Lisa. The crudeness fits his character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, the tar fumes are making me dizzy! Yeah, they'll do that.
jokes.setup: Ralph complains about dangerous work conditions
jokes.punchline: Homer casually acknowledges health hazard without concern
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual workplace negligence - treats child labor safety concerns dismissively

jokes.text: What? This is a travesty! Everyone knows I'm the best actor in this ridiculous school!
jokes.setup: Ralph cast as George Washington over Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's dramatic overreaction and insult to venue
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's theatrical ego - self-aggrandizing while insulting the institution

jokes.text: Rex's theatrical outburst over casting: 'Someone's gotten to you, you deceitful cow!'
jokes.setup: Rex loses the George Washington role to Ralph
jokes.punchline: His over-dramatic accusation of corruption in elementary school theater
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing child actor's inflated sense of drama. The insult 'deceitful cow' is perfectly theatrical and inappropriate.

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum's corruption revealed through car boot removal signal
jokes.setup: Ralph gets the lead role unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that Wiggum bribed his way to get Ralph the part
jokes.explanation: Clever visual storytelling showing police corruption in school theater casting. The signal system implies routine corruption.

jokes.text: Ralph's note in Malibu Stacy car: 'Look in the tunk' (meaning trunk)
jokes.setup: Lisa finds expensive gift from Ralph
jokes.punchline: Ralph's misspelling of 'trunk' as 'tunk' in his note
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph character moment with innocent misspelling. The phonetic spelling shows his child logic and adds charm.

jokes.text: Bart's absurd Krusty Home Pregnancy Test reference
jokes.setup: Bart claiming to be bigger Krusty fan than Lisa
jokes.punchline: Cites ownership of inappropriate/impossible Krusty-branded medical device
jokes.explanation: Absurd commercial tie-in joke that satirizes inappropriate product licensing. The specific absurdity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Bart's escalating sacrifice offers for Krusty tickets ending with 'You don't wanna know how far I'll go'
jokes.setup: Bart offering to make romantic sacrifices disguised as Lisa
jokes.punchline: His final ominous suggestion of unlimited commitment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with increasingly disturbing implications. Bart's desperation for tickets drives the dark humor.

jokes.text: Sure, it is, honey. You do mean stealing, don't you?
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if it's right to take things from people you don't like
jokes.punchline: Homer encourages theft while seeking clarification
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual criminality - treats theft as acceptable while seeking technical confirmation

jokes.text: Homer's terrible parenting advice: 'Your conscience? Lisa, don't let that pushy little weenie tell you what to do.'
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if it's right to take gifts from people you don't like
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses conscience as annoying interference with selfish desires
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer parenting philosophy - personifying conscience as 'pushy little weenie' is perfectly absurd and memorable. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Ralph: 'I am so glad you cho-cho-chose to come' at Krusty show
jokes.setup: Ralph using his favorite phrase from earlier valentine
jokes.punchline: His continued innocent joy over the train pun
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the valentine moment. Ralph's continued delight in the pun shows his innocent character consistency.

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum's confusion about porn bust: 'Uh, yeah... that's just what it is-- a bust'
jokes.setup: Wiggum caught at adult theater, questioned if it's a police bust
jokes.punchline: His nervous attempt to play with double meaning of 'bust'
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's nervous wordplay while caught in compromising situation. The pun works but his delivery reveals his discomfort.

jokes.text: Krusty's political joke: 'I campaigned for the other guy, but I voted for you!'
jokes.setup: Krusty addressing the President in audience
jokes.punchline: Admits to campaign disloyalty but voting loyalty
jokes.explanation: Standard political humor about public vs. private political positions. Works as celebrity pandering comedy.

jokes.text: Krusty's dated reference: 'I started this show so long ago, the ayatollah only had a goatee'
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining how long he's been on the air
jokes.punchline: Specific dated reference to Iranian leader's changing facial hair
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference joke about passage of time. The specific detail about facial hair evolution is funny and memorable.

jokes.text: Krusty's bombing with crowd: 'What's the matter? Don't you people read the papers?'
jokes.setup: Krusty's topical joke gets no reaction from audience
jokes.punchline: His defensive blame of audience for not understanding reference
jokes.explanation: Good comedian bombing material. Shows Krusty's ego and tendency to blame audience rather than accept failure.

jokes.text: Ralph offers Lisa ice cream, Homer says 'Send it this way, boy' and Ralph drops it
jokes.setup: Ralph politely offering Lisa ice cream at show
jokes.punchline: Homer's greedy intervention causing spillage
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfishness ruining romantic gesture - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Uh, the script says I'm supposed to bonk you with this. I wouldn't. Right on.
jokes.setup: Krusty reads stage direction about hitting sidekick
jokes.punchline: Militant sidekick threatens violence, gets respectful response
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - militant sidekick changes traditional slapstick power structure

jokes.text: Ralph's innocent declaration on TV: 'I love Lisa Simpson, and when I grow up, I'm going to marry her!'
jokes.setup: Krusty interviewing Ralph and Lisa on live television
jokes.punchline: Ralph's public declaration of love creates maximum embarrassment for Lisa
jokes.explanation: Crucial emotional moment that sets up the episode's climax. Ralph's innocent sincerity makes Lisa's situation more painful.

jokes.text: Lisa's brutal rejection: 'Now, you listen to me! I don't like you! I never liked you! And the only reason I gave you that stupid valentine is 'cause nobody else would!'
jokes.setup: Lisa's mounting embarrassment from Ralph's public declaration
jokes.punchline: Her complete emotional destruction of Ralph on live television
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional climax that's both cringe comedy and genuine drama. Lisa's complete honesty is both cruel and understandable.

jokes.text: Bart's cruel observation: 'Watch this, Lise. You can actually pinpoint the second when his heart rips in half.'
jokes.setup: Ralph's visible heartbreak after Lisa's rejection
jokes.punchline: Bart's clinical analysis of emotional devastation with VCR replay
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons moment combining dark humor with technical precision. The clinical observation of heartbreak is both funny and horrifying. Became cultural reference.

jokes.text: At least you guys are my friends.
jokes.setup: Ralph seeks comfort after public humiliation
jokes.punchline: Confides in ducks who immediately abandon him
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking Ralph moment - seeks comfort from nature, gets rejected by animals too

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum's misguided parenting: 'Son, I know just how you feel-- You got a great little girl, and the world's your oyster. No, Dad. She made a fool out of me. Hey, come to think of it, she did.'
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to console heartbroken Ralph
jokes.punchline: His complete reversal from comfort to agreement that Ralph was foolish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum parenting - starts supportive but quickly agrees with the harsh assessment. Shows his complete lack of emotional intelligence.

jokes.text: Now, where did I put my badge? Hey, that duck's got it! Oh, come on. Come on. Give it back. I need it. Aww, keep it.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum threatens police action against Lisa
jokes.punchline: Loses badge to duck, gives up immediately
jokes.explanation: Wiggum incompetence - loses authority symbol to waterfowl, accepts defeat

jokes.text: Duck stealing Chief Wiggum's police badge and his resignation to it
jokes.setup: Wiggum loses his badge while feeding ducks with Ralph
jokes.punchline: Duck takes the badge and Wiggum gives up trying to retrieve it
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Wiggum's incompetence even with wildlife. His casual resignation to losing authority symbol fits his character.

jokes.text: Wiggum's corrupt traffic stop fears: 'They are? Oh, no! Have they set a date?'
jokes.setup: Citizen threatens that honest citizens will stand up to corrupt cops
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's literal interpretation asking for a specific date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum literal-mindedness combined with acknowledgment of his own corruption. The calendar-based interpretation is absurdly specific.

jokes.text: Skinner's self-blame revelation: 'So Mother was right. It was my fault.'
jokes.setup: Groundskeeper explains why Fire Drill Follies failed (everyone left during opening fire drill)
jokes.punchline: Skinner's immediate acceptance that his mother was right to blame him
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment showing his automatic deference to maternal judgment even in obvious situations.

jokes.text: My God, man. I've watered her down as far as she'll go. I cannot water no more.
jokes.setup: Skinner asks Willie to water down orange drink more
jokes.punchline: Willie's dramatic declaration about dilution limits
jokes.explanation: Willie's theatrical passion applied to mundane task - treats beverage dilution as moral crisis

jokes.text: Bart as John Wilkes Booth: 'I am not a butt!' causing chaos
jokes.setup: Bart playing Lincoln's assassin in school play
jokes.punchline: His defiant declaration about his name causes audience panic
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor playing on Booth's name. Works as child's interpretation but relatively simple wordplay.

jokes.text: Bart's Terminator reference as assassin: 'Hasta la vista, Abie'
jokes.setup: Bart as John Wilkes Booth about to shoot Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Modern action movie catchphrase applied to historical assassination
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor mixing historical drama with modern action movie. Fits Bart's character but predictable reference.

jokes.text: You're next, Chester A. Arthur!
jokes.setup: Bart continues violent rampage as Booth
jokes.punchline: Threatens obscure president with assassination
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation - references genuinely obscure president shows writers' historical knowledge

jokes.text: I asked for no broth. Away with you, lest my cane find your backside!
jokes.setup: Ralph performing as dying Washington
jokes.punchline: Threatens servant with violence using presidential cane
jokes.explanation: Ralph's unexpected dramatic intensity - transforms from sweet child to commanding president

jokes.text: Groundskeeper Willie's emotional breakdown: 'I didna cry when me own father was hung for stealin' a pig... but I'll cry now.'
jokes.setup: Ralph's performance moving the audience to tears
jokes.punchline: Willie's comparison of emotional moments reveals dark family history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment combining emotional vulnerability with casually revealed family tragedy. The contrast between stoicism and current emotion is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Lisa's 'Let's bee friends' valentine with bee picture
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to repair relationship with Ralph after devastating rejection
jokes.punchline: Her friendship valentine uses bee/be pun that Ralph appreciates literally
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback to the train valentine format. Shows Lisa's growth and Ralph's consistent appreciation for visual puns.

jokes.text: Radio DJ callback: 'Doggone it!' - plays wrong song again
jokes.setup: Episode ends with same radio DJ from beginning
jokes.punchline: He makes the same mistake again, playing wrong song for occasion
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend callback to opening radio mistake. Creates structural completeness but not particularly strong on its own.

tmdb_overview: Chief Wiggum's son has a crush on Lisa after she gives him a Valentine out of pity.
insight: Ralph's Valentine obsession drives 19 jokes—his sweetest cringe hour yet.
